vision.rationale: README.md provides comprehensive project vision including the dual purpose (audit snapshot + TypeScript port), the cross-harness consistency model, and the relationship to upstream GSD. AGENTS.md adds context about what gets published and the ongoing TS port. Note: README still references 'zero runtime dependencies' for upstream GSD, but the pi-gsd port itself now has dependencies.

architecture.rationale: Multiple detailed architecture documents exist: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md covers the hardlink hook system and install pipeline; .gsd/JS_MODULE_ARCHITECTURE.md provides the complete module dependency graph and data flows; COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md document cross-harness structure; README.md §4 and §7 detail the consistency model and directory layout.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md explicitly lists conventions (Tier-1/Tier-2 rules, hook management, command prefix rules, published content scope, never-touch files), commands, directory structure, pre-commit hooks, and release process. tsconfig.json codifies TypeScript conventions (strict mode).

vision.rationale: README.md clearly articulates the project vision: a post-install snapshot of GSD v1.30.0 across 8 AI coding harnesses for cross-harness audit, documentation, drift analysis, and as a reference for contributors. AGENTS.md adds the TypeScript porting goal and npm publishing scope. Both files explicitly state what the repo is and is not.

architecture.rationale: Multiple architecture documents exist: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md provides the full hook hardlink model, 3-stage install pipeline, and runtime self-detection patterns. COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md covers command architecture. HARNESS_DIFF.md covers cross-harness binary/workflow divergence. .gsd/JS_MODULE_ARCHITECTURE.md covers the module dependency graph. README.md §§3-7 document the two-tier consistency model and directory layout. The src/ directory reveals the TypeScript module architecture mirroring the CJS layout.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md contains an explicit 'Conventions' section with 6 numbered rules covering tier-1 byte identity, tier-2 canonical editing, hook hardlink discipline, command prefix rules, published content scope, and file-touch restrictions. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md §6 provides detailed DO/DON'T contributor rules specifically for hook development. src/cli.ts header comment also states a fidelity principle for the TypeScript port.

architecture.rationale: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md provides detailed architecture of the hook model (3-stage install pipeline, runtime self-detection, harness coverage table). AGENTS.md describes the directory structure (Tier-1/Tier-2 modules, canonical sources, src/ layout). COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md provide additional architectural references. The .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts and installPiExtension() in postinstall.js reveal the pi extension architecture. src/cli.ts shows the full command routing architecture with lazy module loading and SKIP_ROOT_RESOLUTION set.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has an explicit 'Conventions' section with 7 rules covering Tier-1 byte-identity, Tier-2 canonical editing, hook model, command prefix discipline, published content restrictions, never-touch files, and model-profiles validation workflow. It also has 'Testing' and 'Commands' sections defining mandatory pre-publish steps. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has a 'Rules for Contributors' section. architecture.rationale: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md provides detailed architecture of the hook model (3-stage install pipeline, runtime self-detection, harness coverage table). The .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts and installPiExtension() in postinstall.js reveal the pi extension architecture. src/cli.ts shows the full command routing architecture with lazy module loading, SKIP_ROOT_RESOLUTION set, and the new isPiDirArg() pattern for pi-specific argument handling in extract-messages.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has an explicit 'Conventions' section with 7 rules covering Tier-1 byte-identity, Tier-2 canonical editing, hook model, command prefix discipline, published content restrictions, never-touch files, and model-profiles validation workflow. It also has 'Testing' and 'Commands' sections defining mandatory pre-publish steps. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has a 'Rules for Contributors' section. vision.rationale: README.md contains a comprehensive vision: pi-gsd as an unofficial port of GSD v1.30.0 for pi and 7 other harnesses, delivering a structured six-step software delivery framework. The 'What You Get' table, workflow diagram, comparison table with GSD v1.30.0 (all features now ✔️), and data compatibility section articulate the vision clearly. Updated to reflect all completed features.

architecture.rationale: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md provides detailed architecture of the hook model (3-stage install pipeline, runtime self-detection, harness coverage table). AGENTS.md describes the directory structure (Tier-1/Tier-2 modules, canonical sources, src/ layout). COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md provide additional architectural references. The .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts reveals the pi extension architecture. The refactored src/cli.ts (~1077 lines) shows the complete command routing architecture with lazy module loading, SKIP_ROOT_RESOLUTION set, local stdout interception closure with @file: support, workstream auto-detection, inline harness detection via __dirname regex, and isPiDirArg() convention. src/lib/core.js centralizes core utilities.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has an explicit 'Conventions' section with 7 rules covering Tier-1 byte-identity, Tier-2 canonical editing, hook model, command prefix discipline, published content restrictions, never-touch files, and model-profiles validation workflow. It also has 'Testing' and 'Commands' sections defining mandatory pre-publish steps. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has a 'Rules for Contributors' section. TODO.md stated format compatibility is a hard constraint. The gsd-hooks.ts source encodes the non-blocking guarantee. The refactored src/cli.ts encodes gsdError(), lazy import, isPiDirArg(), and parseNamedArgs() conventions.

vision.rationale: README.md contains a comprehensive vision: pi-gsd as an unofficial port of GSD v1.30.0 for pi and 7 other harnesses, delivering a structured six-step software delivery framework. The 'What You Get' table, workflow diagram, comparison table with GSD v1.30.0 (with done/in-progress/aspirational feature tracking), and data compatibility section all articulate the vision clearly. The comparison table distinguishes ✔️ (done), ⚡ (enhanced beyond original), and 🚧 (in progress) features.

architecture.rationale: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md provides detailed architecture of the hook model (hardlink model, 3-stage install pipeline, runtime self-detection, harness coverage). AGENTS.md describes directory structure and canonical source locations. COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md provide additional architectural references. src/lib/schemas.ts defines the .planning/ data model architecture. .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts and installPiExtension() in postinstall.js reveal the pi extension architecture. src/cli.ts shows the full command routing architecture with lazy module loading. package.json reveals production dependency architecture (@toon-format/toon, commander, jsonpath-plus, zod).

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has an explicit 'Conventions' section with 6 rules: canonical CJS modules, hardlinked hooks, command prefix discipline, published content restrictions, never-touch files, model-profiles validation workflow. Also has 'Commands' and 'Testing' sections defining mandatory steps. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has contributor rules. TODO.md explicitly states .planning/ format compatibility is a hard constraint. src/lib/schemas.ts encodes the permissive-by-design principle (all .passthrough()). gsd-hooks.ts source encodes the non-blocking guarantee principle.

vision.rationale: README.md contains a comprehensive and accurate vision as of v1.4.1. The comparison table now correctly reflects implementation status — all ✔️ and ⚡ items are genuinely done. The 'What You Get' table, workflow diagram, skills list, CLI reference, and model profiles sections articulate the project's scope. No aspirational drift observed.

architecture.rationale: Multiple architecture sources: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md (hook model, hardlink architecture, 3-stage install), AGENTS.md (directory structure, canonical source locations), COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md (command and harness architecture), src/lib/schemas.ts (.planning/ data model with Zod), .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts (pi extension with 3 lifecycle hooks + 5 instant commands via pi.registerCommand(), typed GsdProgress/GsdStats/GsdHealth/GsdState interfaces, runJson<T>() helper, nextSteps()/nextCommand() pure functions), package.json (pi.skills + pi.prompts dual discovery, prompts/ in files), scripts/postinstall.js (multi-harness install pipeline), src/cli.ts (command routing with lazy module loading and stdout interception).

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has explicit Conventions section (6 rules: canonical modules, hardlinked hooks, command prefix, published content, never-touch, model-profiles validation). HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has contributor rules. TODO.md encodes the .planning/ format compatibility hard constraint and tracks remaining any-cast work. src/lib/schemas.ts encodes the permissive-by-design principle (all .passthrough()). gsd-hooks.ts encodes the non-blocking guarantee, instant command patterns, and context warning semantics. Ralphi pre-commit hook now enforces standards at commit time.

Notable discrepancy: README says 55 skills and 54 prompts but actual counts in skills/ and prompts/ directories are 62 each.

architecture.rationale: Multiple architecture sources: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md (hook model), AGENTS.md (directory structure, conventions), .gsd/extensions/pi-gsd-hooks.ts (canonical pi extension v1.6.5 with 3 hooks + 5 instant + ~50 alias commands), .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts (old extension retained for reference), .gsd/harnesses/pi/ (harness bundle with gsd-file-manifest.json), scripts/postinstall.js (rewritten installer with HARNESSES array, installPiExtension() version detection, stale cleanup, INIT_CWD), package.json (pi.skills + pi.prompts, tsup build, @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent), src/cli.ts (command routing), src/lib/schemas.ts (.planning/ data model with Zod).

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has explicit Conventions section (6 rules, build command references tsup). HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has contributor rules. TODO.md encodes format compatibility constraint. src/lib/schemas.ts encodes permissive-by-design (.passthrough()). pi-gsd-hooks.ts encodes non-blocking guarantee, instant command patterns, alias command pattern, context warning semantics. 'Extension is owned by pi-gsd' principle encoded in installPiExtension() version detection. Stale cleanup principle encoded throughout postinstall.js.

vision.rationale: README.md contains a comprehensive and accurate vision as of v1.7.0. The comparison table shows /gsd-plan-milestone and /gsd-execute-milestone as ✔️, confirmed by actual workflow files in .gsd/harnesses/pi/gsd/workflows/. All ✔️ and ⚡ items are genuinely implemented. No aspirational drift.

architecture.rationale: Multiple architecture sources: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md (hook model, hardlink architecture, install pipeline), AGENTS.md (repository layout with .gsd/harnesses/, pi native package discovery), package.json (pi.extensions + pi.prompts, tsup build), scripts/postinstall.js (HARNESSES config, INIT_CWD, installPiExtension with settings.json update and stale cleanup, prompt stale cleanup), .gsd/harnesses/pi/gsd-file-manifest.json (9 workflow files), .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts (3 lifecycle hooks + 5 instant commands, execAsync-based runJson<T>()), src/cli.ts (command routing with tsup), src/lib/schemas.ts (.planning/ data model with Zod), COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has explicit Conventions section (6 rules: canonical modules, pi extension copy model, command prefix, published content, never-touch, model-profiles validation), Known Issues/Tech Debt (3 remaining any casts), mandatory commands, testing approach, hardlinked hooks, pi native package discovery sections. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has contributor rules. TODO.md encodes remaining work. src/lib/schemas.ts encodes permissive-by-design principle. gsd-hooks.ts encodes non-blocking guarantee. Ralphi pre-commit hook enforces standards.

vision.rationale: README.md contains a comprehensive and accurate vision as of v1.7.0. The comparison table shows /gsd-plan-milestone and /gsd-execute-milestone as ✔️. All ✔️ and ⚡ items are genuinely implemented. The README.md change in this reseed was purely cosmetic: whitespace/alignment adjustments to two rows in the comparison table (lines 142-143 for `ui-brand` context modularity and `syncReferenceToCore` utility rows), no substantive content changes.

architecture.rationale: Multiple architecture sources: HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md (hook model, hardlink architecture, install pipeline), AGENTS.md (repository layout with .gsd/harnesses/, pi native package discovery), package.json (pi.extensions + pi.prompts, tsup build), scripts/postinstall.js (HARNESSES config, INIT_CWD, installPiExtension with settings.json update and stale cleanup, prompt stale cleanup), .gsd/harnesses/pi/gsd-file-manifest.json (9 workflow files), .gsd/extensions/gsd-hooks.ts (3 lifecycle hooks + 5 instant commands, execAsync-based runJson<T>()), src/cli.ts (command routing with tsup), src/lib/schemas.ts (.planning/ data model with Zod), COMMAND_PREFIX_MAP.md and HARNESS_DIFF.md.

principles_guidelines.rationale: AGENTS.md has explicit Conventions section (6 rules: canonical modules, pi extension copy model, command prefix, published content, never-touch, model-profiles validation), Known Issues/Tech Debt (3 remaining any casts), mandatory commands, testing approach, hardlinked hooks, pi native package discovery sections. HOOKS_ARCHITECTURE.md has contributor rules. TODO.md encodes remaining work. src/lib/schemas.ts encodes permissive-by-design principle. gsd-hooks.ts encodes non-blocking guarantee. Ralphi pre-commit hook enforces standards.
